title: Honor explicit 0-value collection filters (Greptile P1, re-review)
rationale: The collection command gated each 0/1 filter flag (own, rated, played, wishlist, want, trade, stats, brief) on `if flagX != 0`, which cannot distinguish the Go zero value (flag unset) from an explicit `--own 0` (user wants only not-owned items). BGG treats `own=0` as a real filter, so `collection --username foo --own 0` silently returned all items instead of only not-owned ones.

changes.summary: Replaced the `if flagX != 0` gates with `if cmd.Flags().Changed("x")` for own, rated, played, wishlist, want, trade, stats, and brief, so an explicitly-set 0 is sent to BGG and an unset flag is omitted. String flags (username, subtype, excludesubtype, modifiedsince) are unaffected.
changes.validated_outcome: go build/vet/test pass; `--own 0` now sends own=0; unset flags still omitted (no change to existing behavior or golden output).
